ground_truth.title: Latest Cloud Computing News | CIO Dive
ground_truth.main_content: Cloud computing news and articles covering AI infrastructure, cloud platforms, and enterprise technology. Recent stories include: House passes bill to fast-track AI infrastructure projects; IBM releases SAP migration tool as deadline looms; Oracle defends infrastructure spending amid AI demand; Snowflake deepens partnerships for AI use; SAP deploys EU AI Cloud for data sovereignty; AWS and Google link up for multicloud deployments; Nvidia shows strong AI demand; EU plans digital regulation pullback; EU regulators launch cloud market probes; SAP addresses competition concerns; Broadcom launches open ecosystem for VMware Cloud Foundation; Regulatory risks push cloud leaders to alter infrastructure; Banks turn to AI supervisors as agent use surges; SAP and Snowflake partner; Clorox blames ERP overhaul for sales drop; OpenAI and AWS sign $38B compute deal; AWS revenue jumps to $33B; Microsoft reports $49B cloud revenue; Amazon cuts 14K jobs in AI reorganization; 4 tips for evaluating cloud AI providers; SAP focuses on cloud gains and agentic AI; AI shapes cloud spend; AWS outage puts spotlight on IT durability.
with: The SPEED Act aims to cut red tape in order to meet soaring energy demand and growing competition in the global AI race, according to the bill's sponsors.
Fueled by generative and agentic AI, the IBM Consulting Application Management Suite for SAP aims to simplify the transition to S/4HANA.
The company raised its fiscal year capital investments forecast by $15 billion as its cloud backlog surpassed $500 billion.
Microsoft Azure will also join the service in 2026. It's the latest hyperscaler move to ease interoperability.
AI adoption is increasing enterprise cloud spending, pushing CIOs to balance cost management with multicloud strategies and energy-intensive infrastructure.
without: Don't miss tomorrow's tech industry news
Subscribe to CIO Dive for top news, trends & analysis
